summary: reconcileStreetsAction applies position updates/inserts one statement at a time against the non-deferrable UNIQUE (route_id, position) constraint, so any reordering edit raises a duplicate-key error mid-transaction; verified: the PR's own RouteBuilderControllerSpec 'replace the street list in place' test fails with this error.
failure_scenario: Save a route [A], then PUT with streets [B, A]: INSERT of B at position 0 while A still holds position 0 → PSQLException 23505 → 500; in the UI, load a saved route, Reverse, 'Update route' → generic save-error toast; only pure end-appends/tail-removals succeed.

summary: #reconstructPath decides traversal direction with an absolute test (entry node within 15 m of coords[0]) instead of comparing distances to both endpoints, so short streets (10-15 m straight-line, up to ~25 m with node-merge drift) traversed against coordinate order are misclassified flip=false; curved streets with near-adjacent endpoints (hooks) can be long yet still misfire.
failure_scenario: Route passes through a 12 m connector entered at its last coordinate: distanceM(entryNode, coords[0]) = 12 < 15 → flip=false; arrows point backwards, polyline discontinuous at that street, wrong reverse flag saved, Explore walks it the wrong direction.

summary: #reverseRoute mutates #waypoints without pushing an UndoStack entry, so Undo after Reverse pops a stale 'waypoint' entry and removes waypoints[length-1] — after reversal that's the original start point: the reverse is not undoable and undo deletes the wrong end.
failure_scenario: Build A→B→C, click 'Reverse route direction' (now C→A), press Ctrl+Z expecting to undo the reverse → waypoint A (original start, now finish) silently deleted, leaving C→B still reversed; repeated undos dismantle the route from the original-start side.

summary: DirectionsPanel's Start field is wired to plain #addWaypoint, which always appends to the route's tail once any waypoint exists — typing a new Start address after the route has begun extends the END to that address while the field labels it 'Start'.
failure_scenario: User clicks out A→B, then types address C into the Start field to move the start → route becomes A→B→C with C as the finish flag; the Start field shows the typed C label while the actual start remains A — panel and route disagree, user intent inverted.

summary: For guests, #updateSavedRoute's success path calls #savedRoutes.refresh(), but guest refresh only re-reads localStorage, written solely by recordGuestRoute on first save — stored distanceMeters/encodedPolyline/regionName never updated after a PUT, so the card's thumbnail/distance/time stay permanently stale.
failure_scenario: Anonymous user saves a 1 km route, reopens, extends to 3 km, clicks 'Update route' → server route is 3 km but the 'Your saved routes' card shows 1 km and the old shape on every future visit; copy-link/Explore lead to a route that doesn't match its card.

summary: Streets whose two endpoints merge into one graph node (separation < 10 m: short stubs, small loops) are stored in #features but given no edges, and route() returns no-path when from.nodeKey === to.nodeKey — the graph cannot represent streets that street data and existing saved routes legitimately contain.
failure_scenario: A saved route contains an 8 m street: #waypointsFromStreets puts both leg waypoints on the same node, route() yields no-path and skips it, the route draws without that street ('route-adjusted' toast), and pressing 'Update route' silently deletes the street — and its audit_task_user_route progress rows — from the saved route.
